subject: [PATCH v4 08/10] fuse: support registered buffers as buffer pools in io-uring

2026-06-12.patch_summary: This patch adds support for registered buffers as buffer pools in io-uring, allowing servers to use an io-uring registered buffer as the buffer pool and eliminating the overhead of pinning/unpinning user pages per i/o request. The server must register the payload buffers through io-uring with the FUSE_URING_REGISTERED_BUFFERS init flag set, and follow standard protocol for using registered buffers. This patch also includes kernel support for registered buffers advertised to userspace via the FUSE_HAS_IO_URING_BUFPOOL init flag. Benchmarks show a 10% throughput improvement for direct randreads and other improvements for different types of reads.
